cover_letter:
Hi there,

I'm reaching out about the Backend Engineer role at Reo.Dev. I've been building backend systems for the past couple years, mostly with Node.js and TypeScript, and the work you're doing around revenue intelligence for DevTool companies caught my attention.

Most recently I've been working on distributed event-driven pipelines using BullMQ and PostgreSQL on AWS ECS — handling over a million events per hour with dead-letter queues and retry logic. I also built out idempotent payment integrations and REST APIs for a tourism platform, and I've done quite a bit of work with pgvector for similarity search. I like owning things end to end, from architecture decisions through to shipping and iterating based on what actually works.

What draws me to Reo.Dev is the startup environment and the caliber of your customers. I've been working in fast-paced settings where priorities shift and you have to be proactive about solving problems. I'm comfortable with that. I also think the AI angle you're exploring is interesting and I'd enjoy working with a team that's building something with real traction.

Happy to chat more if you think there's a fit.

Best,
Ahmed
email_body:
Hi,

I wanted to apply for the Backend Engineer position at Reo.Dev. I've attached my resume for your review — I've spent the last couple years building scalable backend systems with Node.js, PostgreSQL, and event-driven architectures on AWS.

You can also find more about my work at https://kennycandra.dev and on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ahmed-abdelrahman-89bbb3203/

I'd love to learn more about the role and the team. Let me know if you'd like to chat.

Thanks,
Ahmed
